feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Integration Suite
feed.subtitle: SAP Integration Suite Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Missing IBAN Services from SAP BTP Service Entitlement / Discovery Center
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Few months back IBAN Services was available as part of SAP BTP service. But recently I don't see this available now in Discovery Center / SAP BTP service entitlement.

I have captured the link and screenshot of the service few months back. But it's not opening now. Is this service decommissioned by SAP, please suggest further.

SAP Discovery Center Service - IBAN service (cloud.sap)
Inital Setup | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Facing issue while Decoding Base64 Encoded PDF in CPI
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I am trying to decode a Base64 Encoded pdf in CPI with the standard Base64 Decoder but it looks like the Decoder generating faulty PDF as after saving the content as .pdf it is not opening , showing document is damaged and can't open the file.

I tried to Decode the same Base64 string on Notepad++ and saved the decoded content as .pdf. The file is opening perfectly.

Even I tried replacing the Standard Decoder with Groovy but issue still persists, Please suggest how to resolve this.

 

Regards,

Arvik

entry.title: SAP Approuter Headers using Private Link Service for Azure Blob Storage
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I have a question in regards to the SAP Approuter (https://www.npmjs.com/package/@sap/approuter) and its handling of the "Authentication" header when sending backend requests via the SAP Private Link Service.

Use Case Explanation (see attached picture for better unterstanding):

In a recent implementiation in a customer project, I was given the task to implement PLS for writing Data to an Azure Blob Storage by use of the SAP Integration Suite. The policies within Azure only allowed Entra-Id based authentication on the Storage Account, so using Access Keys or SAS was not even a considerable option. A Service Prinicple was the way to go here. To have the endpoint of the approuter secured, we implemented an XSUAA which authenticates the Integration Suite to call the approuter endpoint (via x-approuter-authorization Header), however the JWT Token for access to the Blob had to be part of the HTTP Call as well (this must be passed with the standard "Authentication"-Header).

Problem/Question:

It took me a while to find out why the Authentication-Header never appeared in the approuter, until I found out, that the SAP approuter by default deletes this header before sending the request to the backend. On the offical documentation it says because of "security reasons" (see section "Headers"). I would like to know what are these reasons in particular? From my point of view this blocks the support of entra-id-based authentication on blob storages in Azure, which is - btw - the only officially recommended authentication mechanism by Microsoft.

For this setup to work, a manual modification of the approuter was necessary, which is not ideal, especially because it is also not clear why the authetication header is being deleted in the first place.

I would highly appreciate if someone could give an explanation, to see if or how a workaround can be achieved.

Many thanks and Best Regards

entry.title: Error in a script to store a column in CSV payload and assign in property
entry.content:
I have written a script to store a column of csv payload in a property but getting the following error. any help will be appreciated.

 

import com.sap.it.api.mapping.*;
import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;
import java.util.HashMap;

def Message processData(Message message) {

def csvpayload = message.getpayload(java.lang.String.class)

def csvRows = csvpayload.split('\n')

def lastRow = csvRows[csvRows.size()-1]

def lastRowCoulmns = lastRow.split(',')

def lastCoulmn = lastRowCoulmns[lastRowCoulmns.size()-1]

message.setHeader('lastColumn', lastColumn)

retun message

}

 

Error:

 

java.lang.NoSuchMethodException: No signature of method: com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.processor.MessageImpl.getpayload() is applicable for argument types: (java.lang.Class) values: [class java.lang.String]

entry.title: Deploy on Edge Integration Cell using API
entry.content:
Hello product team,

 


We are creating a CICD tool for the integration suite, and we are using the Cloud Integration APIs to import the artifacts.

 

link
https://api.sap.com/api/IntegrationContent/resource/Integration_Flow

 

To deploy, we are using the /DeployIntegrationDesigntimeArtifact api, however, we have the Edge Integration Cell in our landscape and we would like to deploy to the Edge and the api does not have a parameter to determine the Runtime Profile.

 

Could you help us with this guidance.

 

Thanks

entry.title: iFlow - HTTP operation failed invoking https - 401 Auth Error
entry.content:
Hi,

We are trying to fetch Token from Ariba API using Basic Auth Credentials.

Getting below error.

 

Error Details
com.sap.it.rt.adapter.http.api.exception.HttpResponseException: An internal server error occured: HTTP operation failed invoking https://api.ariba.com/v2/oauth/token with statusCode: 401. The MPL ID for the failed message is: xxxxx
 
 
Steps we performed:
Content Modifier>Message Header:
Create - Authorization - Constant - Value: Basic 12345677
 
Http Connection:
https://api.ariba.com/v2/oauth/token
Query: grant_type=client_credentials
 
It is working fine through Postman.
Could you please help.
 
Thank you.

entry.title: Path Variables in Integration Flow
entry.content:
Hi,

How to Pass Path Variables (Path Parameters) in iflow http connection.

We are building an Ariba API, need to pass ViewTemplate. We tried to pass as below but still get error saying the Template not found.

{
"viewTemplateName": "Test_Re",
"filters":
{
"updatedDateFrom":"2024-10-05T20:30:45Z",
"updatedDateTo":"2024-12-05T20:30:45Z"
}
}

entry.title: SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hi Experts,
In a FTP-FTP scenario i want to get the source and target directory path in a content modifier,is there any ways to get it or any camel expression to get the same?
Thank you.

entry.title: Inquiries about Open Connectors Usage Metrics and Pricing Structure
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have four questions regarding Open Connectors consumption and associated costs:

  • The documentation mentions "Keep in mind that Open Connectors uses cloud credits and may incur additional charges. These are the API requests that use cloud credits." Could anyone kindly clarify what exactly cloud credits are in this context?
  • While the documentation states "You can review your Open Connectors cloud credit usage in your SAP Integration Suite overview page," I am only able to check the usage of message on the page. Does anyone know how to get there to check the cloud credit usage, preferably with screenshots?
  • SAP Discovery Center only displays the pricing structure for Integration Suite. Where can I find specific pricing information for Open Connectors? In particular, I need to understand the correlation between cloud credit usage and actual costs incurred.
  • Related to the previous question, if you have information about the pricing structure specifically for the TDD Edition, I would appreciate those details as well.


For your information, we are currently operating in a TDD environment.

 

Thank you.

entry.title: API Management vs Open Connectors
entry.content:
Technology Q&A

Dear all, as a background I am working on a project where we need to consume some API's and make them available in BTP. We had a meeting with the customers and as a solution we have presented them Integration Flows and API Management. We are not having experience with Integration Flows and API Management so we are currently on a learning curve and trying to learn more about them.

While initiating the project they wanted us to configure Open Connectors(on top of Integration Flows) in order to make their APIs externally visible and be able to be commercialized. 
When I did some research we found that the best option for this purpose was the API Management on top of Integration Flows - as I understood Open Connectors might not be available anymore in the future.

Now the customer is always asking what's the difference between them Open Connectors vs API Management. Can someone please help me to differentiate API Management from Open Connectors and show the advantages/disadvantages ?

Their goal is to have their APIs exposed to a marketplace, have external visibility and in future to be able to commercialize them...Can we help them reach their goal with API Management? Is there a place where the APIs can be exposed in the same way as Open Connectors?
Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Authentication using JWT in SAP API Management
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are in a process of implementing OAuth(client credentials based) authentication to API proxies using the JWT verify policy in SAP API Management as described in this post. We used EntraID as IdP. We have been able to successfully implement and test it, but have an observation though. 

We noticed during testing that token fetched for app registered for Dev environment is also working for authenticating with API proxy in SAP APIM Test environment, which is most likely because both the APIs (dev and test) are registered in a common EntraID. 
But is this how it should work? Is there a way to ensure that token fetched for API in one environment does not works for APIs in another environment?

Any inputs will be appreciated. Thanks!

Regards,

Faisal

entry.title: Integration Suit with OpenConnector Google drive integration
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 
i am following this article to integrate Google drive with integration suit

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/openconnectors-googledrive-integration-made-sim...

At the end when i trying to send request through postman it give 401 Unauthorized error.

 

 

So for that i am providing SAP Process Integration Runtime Instance Client id and secret key as basic authentication.
After that it give 500 Internal server error.

 

Can anyone help me out for that?

 

@Sriprasadsbhat

entry.title: need guidance Google Drive integration With SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
hello everyone,
 i am trying to connect my GDrive with the Integration suite  and I am following this tutorial
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/openconnectors-googledrive-integration-made-simple-with-sap-cpi-sap-cp-ocn/ba-p/13408420

 I successfully connect my Drive with an open connector 

 after that, I create a package and its integration flow .
 while creating flow when we create a content manger and provide its details 

when I am providing User, Organization, and Element in the Source Value the deployment process fails.
when I  am providing only User in the Source Value the deployment process is successful but,
after getting the endpoint when I try to GET/POST in Postman it shows an error.
"500Internal Server Error"
"An internal server error occurred: The MPL ID for the failed message is: AGdQPv6Gze2tZpIGePa0E8wPJlok"

 can anyone please guide me, on how to do that perfectly?

@Sriprasadsbhat

entry.title: How to use API pagination in build action project_SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am working on a process where we create an Action Project and use a destination to get the cost center data from an API.

The action works fine, but there is a large amount of data available, so I am unable to get all the data. I am receiving a timeout error. We can provide inputs such as filtering for the top records, but I want to retrieve all the data. I have read about pagination in the SAP help documents, but I am a bit confused about how to implement it.

I am using this data in form creation and approval workflows for dropdown fields.

It would be greatly appreciated if anyone could help me with this.

Thank you!

entry.title: Why is the Integration Suite Activation failing
entry.content: I am stuck at the same state as shown as this screenshot and it fails every time

entry.title: SAP Integration Content API - Is it possible to view all process direct endpoint value
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

I was exploring the available Integration Content API - https://api.sap.com/api/IntegrationContent/overview, 

to view existing data of deployed package/iflows from our SAP CPI. But couldn't find an API which will extract all values of all process direct adapters of deployed artifact / iflows. Is this API feature available?

I have this requirement to extract this data to document all the endpoints to build a detailed landscape of integrations. Since we are using an Async Pipepline, and uses an interface routing iflow which points the received idoc to multiple process direct destination. Need to identify the which iflow the process direct endpoint is used.

@

entry.title: [CPI-Groovy]: Sort XML Nodes based on the start date
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a requirement to sort the EmpJob nodes based on start dates only when there is sinlge EmpEmployment node and jobInfoNav has multiple EmpJob nodes which contains emplStatusNav/PicklistOption/localeLabel is Active in the ascending order. Eg. I am expecting EmpJob/startDate = 2024-11-01T00:00:00.000 in the first node and then EmpJob/startDate = 2025-01-01T00:00:00.000 in the second node. The payload contains multiple records, if these conditions are not satisfied there should not be any sorting done to nodes.

I have tried with the below-mentioned code but it is not working.

Any help or suggestions would be highly appreciated.

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;
import java.util.HashMap;
import groovy.xml.XmlUtil;
import groovy.util.NodeList
import groovy.util.XmlParser;
import groovy.xml.MarkupBuilder;
import java.util.Date;
import java.text.SimpleDateFormat;
import java.util.Calendar;

def Message processData(Message message) {
    def body = message.getBody(java.lang.String);
    def payload = new XmlParser().parseText(body);
    
        payload.PerPerson.each{rec ->  
        def countofEmpEmployment = rec.employmentNav.EmpEmployment.size()
        if (countofEmpEmployment <= 1){
            def countofEmpJob = rec.employmentNav.EmpEmployment.jobInfoNav.EmpJob.size();
            def EmpJobStatus = rec.employmentNav.EmpEmployment.jobInfoNav.EmpJob.emplStatusNav.PicklistOption.localeLabel.text()
            if(EmpJobStatus.contains("ActiveActive")){
            rec.value = rec.employmentNav.EmpEmployment.jobInfoNav.EmpJob.sort{sd ->
                    sd.startDate.text()}
            }
        }
    } 
    message.setBody(XmlUtil.serialize(payload));
    return message;
}

Regards,
Pavan

entry.title: Combining multiple IDOCs into one file at the receiving end in SAP CPI.
entry.content:
Hi, 

I have a requirement to combine multiple idocs into one single file. It is a DESADV.DELVRY03 structure. 

So the output structure should be like;

<DELVRY03>

<IDOC Begin="1">

</IDOC>

</DELVRY03>

For my requirement, it uses Trading Partner Management and in TPM only packet size of 1 supports. So before entering TPM I have used a splitter, and at the receiver end, I have used a custom integration flow and I need to combine the IDOCs using SAP_CorrelationID. 

How is it possible to do the above requirement in SAP CPI?

Thanks,

Radhika

entry.title: Streamlining Business Processes with B2B Integration Factory
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business world, effective communication and efficient data exchange between business partners are crucial. The B2B Integration Factory, a powerful solution designed to simplify and streamline B2B interactions, can transform your business processes. 

In one of our recent projects, I delved deep into the possibilities of the B2B Integration Factory and discovered some insights. At first, it can feel like a lot to take in. But once you get the hang of it, you'll see how useful it can be.  

What is B2B Integration Factory? 

The B2B Integration Factory is a service within SAP that enables organizations to manage their B2B processes more efficiently.

The Partner Directory offers centralized partner management, enabling you to store and manage all partner-specific configurations in one place. This ensures consistency and simplifies the maintenance of partner data. 

Additionally, the B2B Integration Factory provides a wide range of predefined integration content, including mappings, schemas, and connectivity options, which significantly reduce implementation time and effort. 

It is built to handle high transaction volumes and can effortlessly scale to meet your business's growing needs. It supports a variety of sender and receiver communication protocols, including AS2, SFTP, HTTP, and also custom options. 

Last but not least, the B2B Integration Factory ensures secure data exchange with features like encryption, digital signatures, and compliance with industry standards such as EDIFACT, X12, TRADACOMS and more 

 Conclusion 

The B2B Integration Factory in SAP makes B2B interactions easier and more efficient. It cuts down on manual work, reduces mistakes, and speeds up transactions. By using ready-made content, it saves on setup and maintenance costs. Plus, with real-time monitoring and analytics, businesses can keep track of data exchanges and stay competitive in today’s connected world. 

If you would like to learn more, please Contact Proclus - www.proclus-group.com

entry.title: SAP API Management - Set Basic Authentication for API Proxy in Integration Suite
entry.content:
Dear All,

In SAP Integration Suite, Basic Authentication offers a simple way to safeguard your API proxies using a username and password. This blog covers how to configure Basic Authentication for your API proxy in API Management.

Follow the Below Steps to Set Basic Authentication to your API Proxy.

Step 1 : Create a Proxy with your target endpoint (For test purpose i used httpbin URL)

https://httpbin.org/get

 

 



Step 2 : Go to Policies and Assign Basic Authentication in PreFlow Incoming request Stream

 

 Provide the below code in the policy editor:

 

<BasicAuthentication async='true' continueOnError='false' enabled='true' xmlns='http://www.sap.com/apimgmt'>
	<Operation>Decode</Operation>
	<IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>true</IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>
	<User ref='current.username'></User>
	<Password ref='current.password'></Password>
	<Source>request.header.Authorization</Source>
</BasicAuthentication>

 

 

Step 3 : Add Raise Fault Policy in PreFlow Incoming Stream (Next to the Basic Authentication Policy)

 

 

<RaiseFault async="true" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" xmlns="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">
    <FaultResponse>
        <Set>
            <Headers/>
            <Payload contentType="application/json">{"status" : "Error", "messege" : "401 Unauthorized" } </Payload>
            <StatusCode>401</StatusCode>
            <ReasonPhrase>Unauthorized</ReasonPhrase>
        </Set>
    </FaultResponse>
    <IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>true</IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>
</RaiseFault>

 

In RaiseFault Policy ConditionString Provide below Code (Replace "YOUR_USERNAME" & "YOUR_PASSWORD" with your credentials)

 

(current.username != "YOUR_USERNAME")  OR (current.password != "YOUR_PASSWORD")

 

Save and Deploy.

Testing : 

To Test the Basic Authentication Try a GET Call for Your API Proxy in PostMan:

Positive Testing : 

Provide the exact Username and Password given in the Condition String :


Negative Testing : 

Provide an incorrect username and password to verify if access is correctly denied.

Incorrect Username :

Incorrect Password :

Thank You!
